description: You are a financial analyst at an investment bank. Using the workbook 'company_valuation.xlsx' in the ExcelTasks folder on the Desktop, build a complete DCF valuation model for TechFlow Inc. The workbook contains 'Historical_Financials' (2019-2023 income statement and capital structure), 'Assumptions' (growth rates, cost margins, WACC inputs, terminal growth rate), and two empty templates to complete: 'Projected_Financials' (income statement for 2024E-2028E) and 'DCF_Valuation' (free cash flow, WACC, and valuation summary). Fill in both templates using formulas that reference the Assumptions and Historical data. Project the full income statement from Revenue through NOPAT using the provided growth rates and margin assumptions. Calculate unlevered free cash flow for each projection year. Compute WACC using CAPM for cost of equity and the weighted average with after-tax cost of debt. Discount each year's FCF, calculate Terminal Value using the Gordon Growth Model, and complete the valuation bridge from Enterprise Value through Implied Share Price using the 2023 balance sheet data. Use cell references throughout — no hardcoded values. Save the workbook.

metadata.methodology: Discounted Cash Flow with CAPM-derived WACC and Gordon Growth Terminal Value
metadata.data_source: Realistic SaaS company financials; Treasury 10Y yield 4.25% (Jan 2024); Damodaran ERP 5.5%; typical mid-cap tech beta 1.15
